papers.title: Machine Learning for Healthcare: On the Verge of a Major Shift in Healthcare Epidemiology
papers.summary: Foundational review on ML applications in healthcare epidemiology; identifies emerging opportunities and challenges

papers.title: A systematic review of dengue outbreak prediction models: Current scenario and future directions
papers.summary: Review of 99 dengue prediction models from 64 studies; 94.7% used climate data; k-NN and VAR among top performers

papers.title: A Systematic Review of Machine Learning Models for Predicting Outcomes of Stroke with Structured Data
papers.summary: Systematic review of ML for disease outcome prediction methodologies

papers.title: A Review on the Role of Machine Learning in Predicting and Controlling Epidemics
papers.summary: Comprehensive review of ML epidemic prediction and control; covers SVM, RF, DL approaches

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence in Public Health: The Potential of Epidemic Early Warning Systems
papers.summary: Review of AI-driven early warning systems for epidemic preparedness and response

papers.title: Machine Learning and Prediction of Infectious Diseases: A Systematic Review
papers.summary: Systematic review examining ML techniques across multiple infectious diseases

papers.title: Global Infectious Disease Early Warning Models: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
papers.summary: Meta-analysis of 85 EWS models; identifies key predictors and methodological gaps

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence in Early Warning Systems for Infectious Disease Outbreaks
papers.summary: Review of AI-based EWS architectures and their deployment challenges

papers.title: Data-driven Methods for Dengue Prediction and Surveillance Using Real-world and Big Data: A Systematic Review
papers.summary: Review of novel data streams (internet search, social media) combined with ML for dengue

papers.title: Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Dengue Diagnosis Prediction: A Systematic Review
papers.summary: Review of 32 papers; SVM (28.13%) and RF (18.75%) most prevalent for dengue diagnosis

papers.title: Deep Time Series Forecasting Models: A Comprehensive Survey
papers.summary: Survey of DL time series methods including transformers, diffusion, and state-space models

papers.title: Time Series Forecasting with Statistical, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning Methods: Past, Present, and Future
papers.summary: Comprehensive comparison of classical vs ML vs DL forecasting paradigms

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Networks in Time Series: A Systematic Literature Review
papers.summary: Systematic review of GAN applications across time series domains

papers.title: Diffusion Models for Time Series Forecasting: A Survey
papers.summary: Survey of diffusion-based forecasting methods for time series

papers.title: Systematic review of machine learning for dengue outbreak prediction: 2023-2024 Update
papers.summary: Machine learning, especially Random Forest and Neural Networks, outperforms ARIMA. Identifies temperature, humidity, and rainfall as critical predictors. Highlights lack of standardized reporting.

papers.title: Dengue Forecasting Models: A Systematic Review and Network Meta-Analysis of Relative Performance
papers.summary: Network meta-analysis of models from 2014-2024. Found k-NN achieved highest predictive accuracy among ML, while Deep Learning models showed high potential but lower implementability due to data requirements.

papers.title: Analytical review of environmental factors and machine learning techniques in dengue prediction
papers.summary: Concluded that Random Forest and SVM demonstrated higher accuracy than traditional methods in identifying risk areas based on 2020-2024 data.

papers.title: Over Half of Known Human Pathogenic Diseases Can Be Aggravated by Climate Change
papers.summary: 218 of 375 (58%) infectious diseases aggravated by climatic hazards via 1,006 unique pathways

papers.title: Climate Change and Infectious Diseases: Can We Meet the Needs for Better Prediction?
papers.summary: Identifies critical gaps in climate-disease prediction and calls for integrated frameworks

papers.title: Impact of Climate Change on Human Infectious Diseases: Empirical Results and Projections
papers.summary: Empirical evidence of climate impacts on disease; projects increased burden under warming scenarios

papers.title: Differential Effects of Global and Local Climate Data in Assessing Environmental Drivers of Epidemic Outbreaks
papers.summary: Demonstrates differential utility of global vs local climate data for outbreak prediction

papers.title: Estimating the Threshold Effects of Climate on Dengue: A Case Study of Taiwan
papers.summary: Non-linear threshold effects of temperature and rainfall on dengue transmission in Taiwan

papers.title: Nonlinear Impacts of Climate Change on Dengue Transmission in Mainland China
papers.summary: Non-linear climate-dengue relationships with future projections for mainland China

papers.title: Impact of Recent and Future Climate Change on Vector-borne Diseases
papers.summary: Temperature-dependent development of malaria parasites and dengue viruses; future geographic expansion

papers.title: Climate Change: A Driver of Increasing Vector-borne Disease Transmission in Non-endemic Areas
papers.summary: Climate-driven expansion of vector ranges into previously non-endemic temperate regions

papers.title: Climate Change and Infectious Diseases: From Evidence to a Predictive Framework
papers.summary: Framework connecting environmental variability to disease transmission via host-pathogen-vector interactions

papers.title: Thermal Biology of Mosquito-Borne Disease
papers.summary: Optimal transmission temperatures 25-30°C for Aedes-borne viruses with species-specific variation

papers.title: Climate Change and Vector-Borne Diseases: What Are the Implications for Public Health Research and Policy?
papers.summary: Policy implications of climate-driven disease risk; calls for integrated surveillance

papers.summary: Projects 4,000+ novel cross-species viral sharing events by 2070 driven by range shifts

papers.summary: Emergence of thermotolerant fungal pathogens (Candida auris) under warming conditions

papers.title: Climate Change Dynamics and Dengue Transmission Surge in the Americas (2023-2024)
papers.summary: Documents the exponential surge in cases driven by extreme weather events and optimal temperature/humidity thresholds.

papers.summary: RF achieved AUC 0.9547; 21% fewer errors than Poisson regression, 33% fewer than ARIMA

papers.summary: RF risk maps with >80% observed risk ranks within 80% prediction interval

papers.summary: XGBoost achieved lowest RMSE (918.83) and AUC-ROC 97.1%

papers.summary: XGBoost achieved MAE 89.12, R² 0.83; precipitation and humidity key predictors

papers.summary: LightGBM with 95% accuracy; agricultural land, population density, temperature as predictors

papers.summary: Google search patterns track dengue seasonality with strong correlation

papers.summary: First metapopulation + Kalman filter system for spatial dengue spread in Guangdong

papers.summary: Reporting delays (1-50 weeks) as major forecasting challenge; real-time prediction feasible

papers.summary: WHO guidance on climate-based epidemic prediction for surveillance systems

papers.title: Global Analysis and Prediction of Dengue Using Machine Learning
papers.summary: Global-scale dengue prediction using multiple ML algorithms across countries

papers.summary: Systematic evaluation of ML methods for dengue forecasting across multiple settings

papers.title: Hybrid Machine Learning Approach to Zero-Inflated Data Improves Accuracy of Dengue Prediction
papers.summary: Addresses zero-inflated data challenges in dengue prediction, improving accuracy across spatiotemporal resolutions using hybrid ML models.

papers.title: Comparative study aimed at forecasting dengue incidence in Bangladesh: XGBoost and SARIMA
papers.summary: Found that advanced machine learning models like XGBoost were highly effective for infectious disease forecasting, especially with limited surveillance data in resource-constrained settings.

papers.summary: LSTM integrating dengue cases, climate, and mobility for weekly forecasting

papers.summary: LSTM-ATT with RMSE 1.60, MAE 1.95; captures long-term dependencies

papers.summary: LSTM + SHAP feature selection with lagged climate and spatial neighbor effects

papers.summary: CNN, Transformer, LSTM compared; attention mechanisms improved accuracy

papers.title: Machine Learning Models for Dengue Forecasting in Singapore
papers.summary: Compared traditional state space models, SVM, KNN, against CNN and ConvLSTM. CNN yielded lowest RMSE, and deep hybrid recurrent-convolutional networks outperformed standard DL.

papers.title: Dengue forecasting and outbreak detection in Brazil using LSTM integrating human mobility and climate factors
papers.summary: Underscores the significance of incorporating mobility data, especially in urban centers with high population movement, for enhancing dengue forecasting alongside climate data using Deep Learning.

papers.title: Hybrid deep learning model integrating meteorological data and advanced signal processing (SGMD) with BiLSTM-Attention
papers.summary: Uses data augmentation, SGMD decomposition, and BiLSTM with attention to predict dengue fever trends with high accuracy.
